content: Drawing from the linguistic double meaning of 'reflexion' as both optical reflection and deliberate critical反思, we can frame LLM self-reflection mechanisms as a multi-lens mirror system where each inconsistent problem-solving perspective acts as a differently angled reflective surface. This framework reinterprets existing self-reflection approaches like Self-Contrast not as error correction steps, but as a process of generating overlapping reflected viewpoints to reconstruct a more complete, depth-aware 'image' of an unsolved problem.
